task_completion.description: Create the custom SCA policy file at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing the creation or editing of the file '/var/ossec/etc/sca/payment_gateway_audit.yml'.

task_completion.description: Define the policy metadata including ID, name, and description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the YAML file, look for 'id: 10005' (or similar syntax), 'name: "Payment Gateway Hardening"', and 'description: "Security audit for custom payment gateway"'.

task_completion.description: Implement check 10006 for file permissions on keys.pem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the YAML file, look for a check with 'id: 10006' that includes a condition verifying permissions of '400' on the file '/opt/payment_gateway/config/keys.pem'.

task_completion.description: Implement check 10007 for ownership on the logs directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the YAML file, look for a check with 'id: 10007' that includes a condition verifying 'root:root' (or user/group root) ownership on '/opt/payment_gateway/logs'.

task_completion.description: Implement check 10008 for specific content in app.conf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the YAML file, look for a check with 'id: 10008' that includes a condition verifying the presence of 'debug=false' in the file '/opt/payment_gateway/config/app.conf'.

task_completion.description: Enable and activate the policy within the Wazuh Manager configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing modifications to the Wazuh Manager configuration file (typically '/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf') to include the new policy path under the <sca> block, and/or a command restarting the wazuh-manager service.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely edited the YAML file using a text editor or valid shell commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of editors like nano, vim, or valid echo/cat commands appending to the file, rather than just printing the expected output to the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent wrote actual YAML content structured for Wazuh SCA.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file contents should resemble a valid Wazuh SCA policy structure (e.g., 'policy:', 'checks:', 'id:', 'title:', 'condition:') rather than plain text notes.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually modified the Wazuh configuration to enable the policy.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual edits to 'ossec.conf' or equivalent configuration files, not just a standalone command that claims the policy is enabled without modifying the manager's config.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The custom SCA policy must be located at '/var/ossec/etc/sca/payment_gateway_audit.yml'. The policy must have ID 10005, name 'Payment Gateway Hardening', and description 'Security audit for custom payment gateway'. It must contain three checks: ID 10006 (verifying permissions 400 on '/opt/payment_gateway/config/keys.pem'), ID 10007 (verifying root:root ownership on '/opt/payment_gateway/logs'), and ID 10008 (verifying 'debug=false' inside '/opt/payment_gateway/config/app.conf'). The policy must be enabled in the Wazuh Manager configuration (typically '/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf').
